Huge blocks of glittering ice, of a bluish tint on some of the declivities, stood up on all sides, reflecting the early light of morn. The ascent became very perilous. They were obliged to reconnoiter carefully before making a single step, on account of the crevasses. Wilson took the lead, and tried the ground with his feet. His companions followed exactly in his footprints, lowering their voices to a whisper, as the least sound would disturb the currents of air, and might cause the fall of the masses of snow suspended in the air seven or eight hundred feet above their heads.
[TN-001-065]"No; to-morrow morning, and he must not leave Moel until he sees me. We will return to Dal together."
[VB-001-040]"Monsieur!" said I, angrily.

[ASC-002-038]"Illusions, Mr. Bombarnac! Nothing will happen either to you or me. Wait a bit, I promise you a journey, the most prosaic, the most homely, the flattest--flat as the steppes of Kara Koum, which the Grand Transasiatic traverses in Turkestan, and the plains of the desert of Gobi it crosses in China--"
- His life's ambition is fulfilled at the [[North Pole]].

Smith awakes "in very bad humor," his wife having left for France eight days earlier (YEAR-001-010). He begins his round through the departments: rebuking a novelist whose story "lacks the elements of truth," then passing the hall of fifteen hundred reporters telephoning the world's news (YEAR-001-012, YEAR-001-013).
- [[Impey Barbicane]] — President of the Gun Club and the true head of "Barbicane & Co." (TT-003-045)

Lieutenant Davon's men seized Roch, bound him "before he could offer any resistance," and bore him aboard the moored submersible, which then sank and threaded the underwater tunnel in search of the orifice out of the lagoon. (FF-014-074, FF-014-076)

Fort Reliance is the Hudson's Bay Company post on the Great Slave Lake from which the Arctic expedition of *The Fur Country* departs. Situated on the lake's north-east extremity, about three hundred miles from the Chesterfield inlet of Hudson's Bay, it is the last outpost of settled company life before the journey into the far north. (FC-004-002)
- The Fur Country — Verne's novel of the Arctic drift
id: torres-strait

The region is defined by the Senegal River. It marks a great watershed, for the aeronauts "would have to cross the chain which separates the basin of the Niger from the basin of the Senegal" (FWB-041-002). Where the river falls, "with a breadth of two thousand feet, the Senegal precipitates itself for a height of one hundred and fifty, with a thundering reverberation" (FWB-043-067) — the cataracts of Gouina.
description: 'The capital of Chinese Turkestan and the junction of the Russian and Chinese lines of the Grand Transasiatic railway in The Adventures of a Special Correspondent.'
The experimenter vanished with his experiment. This changed everything; but when the army got upon its feet again, Doctor Ox and his assistant Ygène had disappeared (DOSE-015-007).
